mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending economic contraction, causing investors to downgrade earnings expectations and sell equities, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent industrial production expansion and stable consumer price inflation indicate a benign supply-side environment that supports corporate margins without triggering monetary tightening, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 150 basis points over a 3-year walk-forward period, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During active monetary tightening cycles, rising policy rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as discount rates outpace earnings growth.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of tightening episodes, or the rolling 3-year correlation between rate changes and index returns exceeds +0.20.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands aggregate demand and corporate revenues, directly boosting earnings per share and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market participants price in stronger fundamentals.
failure_condition: The historical correlation between quarterly GDP growth surprises and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.15 over any 5-year window, or forward returns are flat during positive GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly employment gains exceeding 150,000 new jobs indicate robust economic momentum and wage growth, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The trigger produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the predictive information ratio drops below 0.5 over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate and expansions in commercial and industrial credit reflect a self-reinforcing economic upswing that lifts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction model fails to achieve a Sharpe ratio above 0.8 over a 3-year rolling window, or the joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of active periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained deceleration in industrial production during late-cycle expansion phases signals demand exhaustion and capacity overhang, causing investors to rotate out of cyclical equities and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields positive forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the rolling 12-month Rank IC remains consistently above 0.04.
